page: Amazon Web Services r5b.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 3.1 GHz. The system provides 64 virtual CPUs derived from 32 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, but it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory performance, particularly in large block read-only and write operations, both of which rank in the top 25 percent. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all scoring in the bottom 25 percent. Multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench scores, and Passmark CPU metrics fall within the average range. Database operations, such as Redis SET operations and Passmark database tests, also demonstrate average performance. Large language model inference speeds are average for prompt processing but weak for text generation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r5b.16xlarge offers a high-density memory configuration relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high single-core CPU throughput. Given the absence of local storage and GPUs, this instance is best suited for memory-intensive applications such as large-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, data analytics, and enterprise applications requiring substantial RAM.
description: Amazon Web Services r5b.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. It features 64 vCPUs across 32 physical cores, 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps, with no local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks show strong memory bandwidth for large block read and write operations, though single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression tasks represent a clear tradeoff, scoring in the bottom 25 percent. Multi-core processing, database operations, and software compilation benchmarks consistently rank in the average tier. This resource profile makes the r5b.16xlarge highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and large-scale data processing applications that benefit from high memory density over raw single-core compute power.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5b.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 512.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 20 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5b.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 512.0 GB RAM, and 20 Gbps network speed for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high-density DDR4 RAM and strong large-block memory bandwidth for demanding database and caching workloads.

512.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Strong memory bandwidth for large block read and write operations
20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and data analytics
